title: Founding Growth Marketer
location: London (Hybrid HQ, WFH Thu + alt Tue)
key_evidence: Explicit zero-to-one mandate: 'our first Growth Marketer... work directly with founders... turn early signals into repeatable growth playbooks' - direct map to Ryan's first-employee + GTM-from-zero spike ($4.5m presale on $300k, 15x ROI)

Wants full-stack growth: customer research, messaging, campaigns, distribution + 'leverage modern AI and automation tools to analyse, track, report, execute' - hits Ryan's content engine + Claude Code automation

Preferred: 'experience driving measurable growth at an early-stage ENTERPRISE B2B startup' - this is the gap; Ryan's growth is community-led/consumer-crypto, NOT enterprise B2B procurement; partial requirement clear
Domain = AI agents for industrial procurement / supply chain ($3tn TAM) - far from crypto/community; meaningful domain ramp

Tiny seed team: 'first 11 people' (ex-OpenAI/Meta/Revolut/NASA/McKinsey), $5.5M seed led by Sequoia (Jul 2025) - founders/CEO read every application = very high human-screen, near-zero ATS-filter risk
JD: 'Work closely with our CEO to refine ICP' + named interview process (founder call -> skills present -> in-person work day) = warm, human, founder-led pipeline
Salary GBP90-100k + equity + visa sponsorship - well above Ryan's 50k floor; strong comp
Posted 2026-03-08 (~3 months old at run) - aging, may be advanced in pipeline or selective
Hybrid London (WFH Thu + alt Tue) - reasonable cadence; in-person final round (Cambridge commuter manageable)
biggest_lever: Sequoia-backed 11-person team means the CEO personally reads applications - a tailored pitch leading with 'I was the first growth hire who built a repeatable GTM playbook from zero ($4.5m presale)' plus an AI-automation artifact lands directly with the decision-maker, sidestepping the enterprise-B2B gap.
biggest_risk: 'Enterprise B2B early-stage growth' preference vs Ryan's community-led consumer-crypto growth - they may want someone who has sold into enterprise procurement/industrial buyers; domain (supply chain/procurement) is unfamiliar and the role has been open ~3 months (possible selectivity).
recency_note: Ashby posting-api publishedAt=2026-03-08T15:17 (magentic board). 93 days / >13wk old = stale; no repost. Tiny 11-person team limits applicant flood but staleness dominates. Source: api.ashbyhq.com/posting-api/job-board/magentic.
